## 2. Semiconductor Fundamentals and Historical Development

![](/uploads/2025/04/01/9c4715a4f75b27b91c1534af521314a9.jpg)

**[Table 1](#Table1).**
Physical properties of Silicon (Si), Silicon Carbide (SiC) and Gallium Nitride (GaN) [[27](#B27)].

| Electrical Property | Si | 4H-SiC | GaN |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bandgap energy (eV) | 1.1 | 3.26 | 3.4 |
| Thermal Conduction (W/cm·K) | 1.5 | 4.9 | 1.3 |
| Electron Mobility (cm2/V·s) | 1300 | 900 | 900–2000 |
| Saturation drift velocity | 1 × 107 | 2 × 107 | 2.5 × 107 |

![](/uploads/2025/04/01/7fd27dd7e548f861ac720c3c592ad6d9.png)

**[Table 2](#Table2).**
Comparison of Recycling Methods for WBG Semiconductors [[54](#B54),[55](#B55),[56](#B56),[57](#B57)].

| Material/Device | Recycling Method | Efficiency/Yield |
| --- | --- | --- |
| GaN substrates | Laser slicing | No degradation |
| Indium (from LCDs) | Mineral acid leaching | Over 95% recovery |
| Gallium (from GaN LEDs) | HCL leaching after annealing | 99% recovery |
| Selenium  (from photovoltaics) | Closed-space evaporation | 98% for selenium,  100% for gold |

enriched_points.expanded_content: Recent developments in wide-bandgap materials, such as silicon carbide (SiC) and gallium nitride (GaN), enhance efficiency, thermal conductivity, and power density in power electronics applications.

Innovative methods like low-energy epitaxial growth and improved recycling processes are revolutionizing the manufacturing of wide-bandgap semiconductors, promoting sustainability and cost-effectiveness.

enriched_points.point_title: Potential for integration in smart grids
enriched_points.expanded_content: Wide-bandgap semiconductors offer superior performance for smart grid applications, enabling advanced energy management, increased reliability, and support for renewable energy sources in modern electric networks.
